title: Caregiver Account Type Selection - Functional Test Plan
overview: Comprehensive functional testing of the Caregiver Account Type Selection feature on the AT/AD platform. This feature enables users to select 'Caregiver' as their account type during sign-up, routing them to the correct onboarding flow (sign-in for existing users, registration for new users). The test plan covers account type display, selection behavior, routing logic, session persistence, dark mode support, and static content validation.

inScope: Account type options display on sign-up page
Caregiver selection routing to sign-in or registration
All four account type options visibility (PwD, Caregiver, Donor, Assistive Partner)
Role persistence in session and analytics
Static card 'Why choose the Portal' display
Account type switching mid-flow with form reset and warning
Page refresh retaining selected role with active session
outOfScope: Caregiver registration form details (covered in SCRUM-357)
PwD registration by caregiver (covered in SCRUM-281)

testCases.title: Verify all four account type options are displayed on sign-up page
testCases.preconditions: User is not logged in; Sign-up page is accessible
steps: 1. Navigate to the AT/AD platform home page
2. Click on 'Sign Up' button/link
3. Verify the sign-up page loads successfully
4. Locate the account type selection section
5. Verify 'Person with Disability' option is displayed
6. Verify 'Caregiver' option is displayed
7. Verify 'Donor' option is displayed
8. Verify 'Assistive Partner' option is displayed
9. Verify all four options are visually distinct and selectable
expectedResults: Account type selection section is clearly visible
All four account type options are displayed: Person with Disability, Caregiver, Donor, Assistive Partner
Each option is visually distinct with clear labels
No account type is pre-selected by default (or default is clearly indicated)
testCases.postconditions: User is on sign-up page with all account type options visible

testCases.title: Verify 'Why choose the Portal' static card is displayed as per design
testCases.preconditions: User is on sign-up page with account type options visible
steps: 1. Navigate to the sign-up page
2. Locate the 'Why choose the Portal' static card/section
3. Verify the card is visible on the page
4. Verify the card content matches the design specification
5. Verify the card layout, typography, and spacing match design
6. Verify the card is positioned correctly relative to account type options
7. Verify any images or icons in the card render correctly
8. Verify the card is readable and not truncated
expectedResults: 'Why choose the Portal' static card is visible on sign-up page
Card content matches the approved design specification
Layout, typography, and spacing are consistent with design
Card is properly positioned and does not overlap other elements
All images/icons within the card render correctly
Card content is fully readable without truncation
testCases.postconditions: Static card is verified to match design specifications

testCases.title: Verify user cannot proceed without selecting an account type
testCases.preconditions: User is on sign-up page; No account type is selected
steps: 1. Navigate to sign-up page
2. Do NOT select any account type
3. Attempt to click 'Continue' or 'Sign Up' button
4. Verify the system prevents navigation without selection
5. Verify an error message or visual cue prompts user to select an account type
6. Select 'Caregiver' account type
7. Verify the continue button becomes active/enabled
8. Click continue and verify successful navigation
expectedResults: Continue/Sign Up button is disabled or shows error when no account type is selected
Error message or visual cue indicates account type selection is required
User cannot proceed to any onboarding flow without selecting an account type
After selecting an account type, the continue button becomes active
testCases.postconditions: Validation for mandatory account type selection is verified

AC1_account_type_options_display.description: User can see account type options: Person with Disability, Caregiver, Donor, Assistive Partner

AC2_caregiver_routing_signin.description: Selecting Caregiver routes existing user to Caregiver Sign-in

AC3_caregiver_routing_registration.description: Selecting Caregiver routes new user to Caregiver Registration

AC5_static_card_design.description: Static card on Why choose the Portal should be as per the design

AC6_dark_mode.description: Available in Dark mode as well

EC1_switch_account_type_midflow.description: User switches account type mid-flow → form resets with warning

EC2_page_refresh_retains_role.description: Page refresh retains selected role (if session active)

assumptions: Sign-up page is accessible at the QA environment URL
All four account types (PwD, Caregiver, Donor, Assistive Partner) are enabled in QA
Dark mode toggle is available on the sign-up page
Session management is functional in QA environment
Design specifications for 'Why choose the Portal' card are finalized
Caregiver registration flow (SCRUM-357) is at least partially functional for routing verification
dependencies: SCRUM-357: Caregiver Registration flow must be available for routing verification
SCRUM-360: Caregiver Sign-In flow must be available for existing user routing
Dark mode feature must be implemented platform-wide

open_questions_and_clarifications.question: Is there a default pre-selected account type, or must the user explicitly select one?
How does the system differentiate between new and existing users after Caregiver selection?
open_questions_and_clarifications.impact: Affects TC_ACCT_SEL_002 and TC_ACCT_SEL_003 routing logic

open_questions_and_clarifications.question: Is the 'Why choose the Portal' card the same for all account types or does it change based on selection?

priority_order: Highest tests (TC_ACCT_SEL_001, 002, 003) should be executed first as they cover core functionality
High priority tests (005, 006, 007) cover static content, dark mode, and edge cases
Medium priority tests (008, 009, 010) cover persistence, other account types, and validation
Low priority tests (013) cover direct URL access

estimated_effort.critical_path: 4-6 hours for critical path testing only

test_environment_requirements: QA environment with AT/AD platform accessible
Pre-registered caregiver account for existing user tests

metadata.coverage: 100% of acceptance criteria and edge cases covered
